meta.name: Garlic & Herb Roast Chicken
meta.subtitle: Lemon-garlic butter under the skin, herb perfume, crisp skin, juicy meat

source.name: ssa.cuisines.herb_garlic
notes: Canonical SSA Herb & Garlic baseline roast chicken.
Designed for deterministic planning + adaptation (oven roast, lemon/garlic/herbs, olive oil option).

yield.notes: 1 whole chicken (3.5–4.5 lb) yields ~4 hearty portions (plus bones for broth).

required.label: Roasting pan or large oven-safe skillet
Instant-read thermometer
required.notes: Strongly recommended for safe doneness and juiciness.

ingredients.label: Whole chicken
notes: 3.5–4.5 lb preferred. Remove giblets if included.
Pat very dry for best browning.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: Add more if using Diamond Crystal vs Morton's (brand saltiness varies).
Optional: dry brine up to 24 hours for deeper seasoning.

ingredients.label: Unsalted butter, softened
notes: Softened, not melted. Helps bind garlic/herbs and bastes under the skin.

ingredients.label: Extra-virgin olive oil
notes: Optional: brush over skin to encourage browning if butter is mostly under-skin.

ingredients.label: Lemon
notes: Use zest + juice in the butter; optional halves inside cavity for perfume.

ingredients.label: Fresh rosemary, very finely chopped
notes: Rosemary is potent—keep it fine and measured.

ingredients.label: Onion, quartered
notes: Optional: place under chicken (or in pan) to flavor drippings for gravy/sauce.

ingredients.label: Chicken stock or water
notes: Optional: add to pan to prevent scorching and create quick pan sauce.

steps.title: Preheat oven and prep pan
instructions: Heat oven to 425°F.
Place a roasting pan or large oven-safe skillet on the counter. If using onion, scatter it in the pan as a bed.
Optional: add 1/2 cup stock or water to the pan to reduce drippings scorching.

steps.title: Make garlic-herb lemon butter
instructions: In a small bowl, combine softened butter with grated garlic, lemon zest, 1–2 tsp lemon juice, parsley, thyme, rosemary, salt, and black pepper.
Mix until evenly blended and spreadable.

steps.title: Dry the chicken and season under the skin
instructions: Remove giblets (if present) and pat chicken very dry with paper towels (including the cavity).
Gently loosen the skin over the breast and thighs with your fingers (avoid tearing).
Spread most of the garlic-herb butter under the skin over the breasts and thighs. Use the remainder over the outside of the skin.

steps.title: Truss (optional) and place in pan
instructions: Optional: truss the legs with kitchen twine for more even roasting.
Place chicken breast-side up in the pan (on top of onions if using).
Optional: brush a thin film of olive oil over skin for extra browning.

steps.title: Roast until golden, then finish to safe doneness
instructions: Roast at 425°F for 20 minutes to start browning.
Reduce heat to 375°F and continue roasting until the thickest part of the breast reaches 160–165°F and the thigh reaches 175–185°F.
If the skin is browning too fast, tent loosely with foil for the remainder.

steps.title: Rest, carve, and finish with lemon/herbs
instructions: Transfer chicken to a board and rest 10 minutes (carryover cooking + juicier slices).
Carve: remove legs/thighs, separate drumsticks, slice breasts against the grain.
Optional: squeeze a little fresh lemon juice over carved meat and sprinkle extra chopped parsley.

steps.title: Optional quick pan sauce (2 minutes)
instructions: If desired, place the pan on the stovetop over medium heat (careful: hot handle).
Skim excess fat, then simmer drippings (and any added stock) 1–2 minutes.
Add a squeeze of lemon and scrape browned bits; spoon over chicken.

refrigerator.guidance: Cool within 2 hours. Store carved meat in an airtight container.

freezer.guidance: Freeze carved meat (best) in portions with a little drippings; thaw overnight in fridge.

best: Oven 325°F covered with a splash of broth/drippings until warmed
Skillet with lid and a spoon of water/broth

batchNotes: Roast 2 chickens if oven space allows: use extra for wraps/salads/broth.
leftovers: Use for lemony chicken orzo soup

summary: A canonical Herb & Garlic roast chicken: garlic-herb lemon butter seasons under the skin for juicy meat and crisp, fragrant skin.

doneness.guidance: Cook to safe internal temperature. Breast 160–165°F, thigh 175–185°F for tenderness.

handling: Avoid cross-contamination: wash hands, sanitize board/knife after raw chicken.
Cool leftovers quickly; refrigerate within 2 hours.

notes: Salt chicken (and optionally pepper) and refrigerate uncovered 8–24 hours for crisper skin.
Apply garlic-herb butter just before roasting.

variations.label: Spatchcock for faster, more even roasting

notes: Remove backbone and flatten chicken; roast at 425°F until breast 160–165°F (often ~40–50 minutes).
Butter can still go under skin.
Replace butter with 3 tbsp olive oil + 1 tbsp additional oil for brushing skin.
Add 1 tsp Dijon mustard (optional) to help emulsify and adhere.
